topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on the dusty attic floor, a cardboard behemoth overflowing with neatly stacked papers. Liam stared at it, his jaw tight enough to crack. Inside, meticulously organized by subject and date, were every single piece of homework his mother had ever completed for him. A low, guttural sound escaped his throat. He reached out, his fingers trembling as he lifted a crumpled worksheet from the top. The ink seemed to vibrate under his touch. "What in the world are you doing?" he finally managed, his voice dangerously flat. He shoved the box back down with unnecessary force, the sound echoing in the quiet space. The sheer audacity of it made his chest feel constricted, hot and tight.

[story 2]

I found the collection tucked inside an old, forgotten textbook. My hands were shaking so badly I almost dropped the entire stack. Each assignment, every essay, every math problem—it was all there. A deep, internal churning started in my gut, a hot, relentless pressure building behind my sternum. I paced the small room, my footsteps sharp against the floorboards. "This is insane," I muttered, the words tasting like ash. I picked up a history paper, the paper crisp under my fingertips, and slowly, deliberately, I began to tear the corner off. The sound of the tearing paper was shockingly loud in the silence.

[story 3]

When I finally opened the trunk, the sheer volume of completed work hit me like a physical blow. My breath hitched, shallow and rapid. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of the lid. A wave of intense agitation washed over me, making my vision narrow. I didn't move for a long moment, just absorbing the evidence—the proof of this strange, meticulous hoarding. Finally, I slammed the lid shut, the resulting thud vibrating through my palms. "Unbelievable," I hissed, the sound barely above a whisper, yet laced with a dangerous edge. I couldn't seem to look at the papers without feeling a sharp, burning resentment rising in my throat.
